purpose: Phase 3 Actual Files Cockpit for the Epstein Command Center. This data powers direct public-facing links to the file routes readers should open first: official disclosures, congressional records, court records, searchable email archives, and supporting archive/search lanes.
boundary: A document route is a starting point, not a conclusion. Readers should open the file, identify the record type, check the date, read the context, and classify what it supports before drawing a conclusion.

doors.title: DOJ Epstein Disclosures
doors.use: Open the official DOJ disclosure route for government-published Epstein material, redactions, indexes, and release notes.
doors.bestFor: Official file releases, redaction context, government-published source material.

doors.title: House Oversight Epstein Records
doors.use: Open the congressional oversight lane for statements, witness testimony, release announcements, subpoenas, and committee framing.
doors.bestFor: Committee releases, testimony, public letters, oversight movement.

doors.title: CourtListener Epstein Dockets
doors.use: Search court records, filings, motions, civil suits, rulings, habeas arguments, Maxwell-related litigation, and related docket material.
doors.bestFor: Court records, exhibits, litigation timelines, filings and rulings.

doors.title: Jmail Epstein Email Search
doors.use: Search the email-style archive by name, topic, sender, recipient, and date. Treat search results as document leads and verify context.
doors.bestFor: Email trails, correspondence patterns, relationship-frequency signals.

doors.title: WikiLeaks Search: Epstein
doors.use: Search public archive material for Epstein-related references. Treat results as leads and corroborate before using them as claims.
doors.bestFor: Auxiliary search leads, cross-reference terms, archive discovery.

doors.title: FBI Vault Search
doors.use: Search the FBI public vault for released files, historic records, FOIA material, and related investigative records.
doors.bestFor: Released law-enforcement files and archive trails.

doors.title: CIA Reading Room
doors.use: Search declassified CIA reading-room material where intelligence-history context is relevant.
doors.bestFor: Declassified archive searches and intelligence-history context.

doors.title: National Archives Catalog
doors.use: Search U.S. archival records, agency material, photographs, legal records, and historical files.
doors.bestFor: Government archive context and historical record searches.

doors.title: SEC EDGAR
doors.use: Search corporate filings, directors, funds, entities, securities records, and financial relationships that may help map money or institutional access.

doors.title: FARA Records
doors.use: Search foreign-agent registration records, lobbying, representation, and influence disclosures.

doors.title: OpenSecrets
doors.use: Search campaign finance, donors, PACs, lobbying, and political-money patterns.

doors.title: ICIJ Offshore Leaks Database
doors.use: Search offshore entities, intermediaries, and company links. Treat results as leads requiring context and verification.
doors.bestFor: Entity leads, offshore structures, intermediary searches.

howToUse: Start with the official disclosure or court record where possible.
Search names across email, court, oversight, and financial routes separately.
Record the document type before interpreting the meaning.
Separate contact, claim, sworn testimony, settlement, and conviction.
Use repeated appearance across independent routes as a stronger network signal.
Never turn a search result into a criminal finding without court-grade support.
